Mala del Brenta.description: Mala del Brenta was a mafia-type criminal organization founded in Veneto, Italy, around the 1970s, later spreading to northeastern Italy. It was heavily involved in kidnappings, robberies, drug and arms trafficking, money laundering, and murders. The group was severely weakened in the 1990s after the arrest and defection of its main boss, Felice Maniero.

Jacks of Hearts Club.description: A criminal organization that operated in the Russian Empire from 1871 to 1875, specializing in fraud, forgery, and deception. Notable for its members being predominantly Russian nobility and wealthy merchants, including a prince. Compton Executioners.description: A deputy gang within the Los Angeles County Sheriff's Department (LASD) based at the Compton station. 'Ndrina Ferraro.description: The Ferraro are a 'ndrina (organized crime clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Oppido Mamertina, Italy. They are allied with the Raccosta family and part of the local 'locale' of Oppido Mamertina. The clan has been involved in a long-running feud with the Mazzagatti-Polimeni-Bonarrigo clans, resulting in over 30 deaths. In 2016, boss Giuseppe Ferraro was arrested after 18 years as a fugitive.

Shiners.description: Gangs of Irish immigrants active in Bytown (later Ottawa) during the 1830s. Formed after the completion of the Rideau Canal left many Irish unemployed, they used intimidation to create jobs in the timber industry. Their activities escalated into robbery and murder, leading to the Shiners' War. The gangs were largely suppressed by armed patrols formed in 1837.

Genovese crime family.description: The Genovese crime family is an Italian American Mafia crime family and one of the Five Families of New York City. Founded by Charles 'Lucky' Luciano in the 1930s, it is the oldest and largest of the Five Families, with strong influence in New Jersey and other regions. Known for its secrecy and white-collar rackets, it is considered the most powerful Mafia family in the U.S. by the FBI.
